mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive 20d returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5d returns are positive in > 50% of triggered months or the signal shows no statistical significance against a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d returns in > 45% of cases or interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive returns in > 60% of late-cycle contraction months or fails to outperform a zero-forecast baseline in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent month-over-month forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of high-inflation months or the threshold signal shows no incremental predictive value over historical averages.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market health, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined labor strength signals yield negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances or the interaction effect vanishes in rolling window validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during a post-shock recovery phase drives equity risk premium compression and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns are negative in > 55% of high-GDP-growth recovery quarters or the signal fails to achieve a positive information coefficient in regime-specific tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through short-term momentum trading.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of expanding production months or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample periods.
